name: San Marino (Most Serene Republic of San Marino / Serenissima Repubblica di San Marino)
summary: San Marino is a Southern European civil-law republic — the world's oldest extant republic (traditionally dated to 301 CE) and the smallest republic in Europe — whose family-law framework operates under the Civil Code (Codice Civile) drawing on Italian civil-law and ius commune substantive heritage, supplemented by Law 51/1986 on Family Law (Legge sulla Famiglia, comprehensive family-law reform) and Law 49/2014 on Filiation. Parental responsibility (responsabilità genitoriale) and child custody are governed by Law 51/1986 arts. 117-152. The Council of Twelve (Consiglio dei XII) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; constitutional review jurisdiction lies with the Collegio Garante della costituzionalità delle norme. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Commissioner's Court (Tribunale Commissariale). Psychology profession is regulated through the Order of Psychologists of San Marino (Ordine degli Psicologi). San Marino is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the superior-interest-of-the-child standard. San Marino acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 March 2007. San Marino is a Council of Europe member subject to ECHR jurisdiction.

statutory_framework.title: Family Law — Parental responsibility and custody
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Family Law comprehensive reform. Arts. 117-152 govern responsabilità genitoriale and child custody.

statutory_framework.title: Law on Filiation
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal law on filiation reform aligned with European trends.

apex_courts.name: Council of Twelve (Consiglio dei XII)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Collegio Garante della costituzionalità delle norme

professional_regulators.name: Order of Psychologists of San Marino (Ordine degli Psicologi)
professional_regulators.role: Federal professional order for psychologists in San Marino.

anonymisation_convention: Sammarinese family-court decisions are anonymised per Council of Twelve practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Republic of San Marino traditional-foundation + world's-oldest-extant-republic framework
key_developments.description: Republic of San Marino traditionally dated to 3 September 301 CE by Marinus of Arba — substantively distinctive globally world's-oldest-extant-republic framework (1,725+ years through 2026). Foundational pre-Constitution-Statutes-1600 framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Statutes of 1600 + republican-constitutional framework
key_developments.description: Statutes of 1600 (Leges Statutae Reipublicae Sancti Marini) — substantively distinctive globally oldest-still-in-force-constitutional-document framework. Substantive republican-constitutional framework persisting through 425+ years affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Italo-Sammarinese Treaty + 1939-amendments framework
key_developments.description: Italo-Sammarinese Treaty 22 March 1862 establishing San Marino-Italy framework. Substantive 1939 amendments substantively reshaping San Marino-Italy framework during Fascist-era. Foundational pre-WWII framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Bombing of San Marino + WWII-neutrality-attempted framework
key_developments.description: Bombing of San Marino 26 June 1944 by Allied forces during WWII despite San Marino neutrality — substantively distinctive globally bombing-of-neutral-micro-state framework. Substantive WWII demographic-impact framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Law 51/1986 on Family Law
key_developments.description: Federal Family Law comprehensive reform enacted 17 March 1986. Arts. 117-152 govern responsabilità genitoriale and child custody. Substantively significant family-law-codification framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Council of Europe membership
key_developments.description: San Marino joined the Council of Europe 16 November 1988; ECHR became applicable 22 March 1989. Substantively significant Southern European human-rights-framework integration affecting subsequent family-law-jurisprudence.

key_developments.title: San Marino UN membership + UNCRC ratification framework
key_developments.description: San Marino UN admission 2 March 1992 — substantively significant micro-state-UN-membership framework. San Marino ratified the UNCRC 25 November 1991 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration affecting subsequent family-law-modernisation.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 accession
key_developments.description: San Marino acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 March 2007 — substantively significant Southern European Hague-accession framework affecting cross-border-jurisdiction-practice with Italy and other Hague-party states.

key_developments.title: Law 49/2014 on Filiation + same-sex-civil-union 2018 framework
key_developments.description: Federal law on filiation reform enacted Law 49/2014 substantively reforming filiation framework. Subsequent Law 147/2018 substantively distinctive Southern European Catholic-micro-state-same-sex-civil-union framework affecting subsequent family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Autorità Garante per l'Infanzia framework + EU association agreement framework + Italian cross-border family-law cooperation
key_developments.description: Autorità Garante per i Diritti delle Persone con Disabilità e per l'Infanzia institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Law 51/1986 + Law 49/2014 framework. EU association agreement framework negotiations 2023 substantively significant Western European micro-state institutional context. Italian cross-border family-law cooperation substantively significant for cross-border family-law adjudication including dual-nationality cases. Substantively significant Apennine institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Consiglio dei XII + Collegio Garante — superior-interest-of-the-child substantive register + Capitani Reggenti framework
key_developments.description: Council of Twelve (Consiglio dei XII) and Collegio Garante della costituzionalità delle norme continue to develop superior-interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Law 51/1986 + Law 49/2014 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes within Capitani Reggenti (semi-annually-rotating-Heads-of-State) framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: San Marino operates an Italian-civil-law-influenced family-law framework — places San Marino in the Italian-civil-law-tradition cluster with Italy within the corpus.
World's oldest extant republic (traditionally 301 CE) is structurally distinctive within the corpus.
Council of Europe + ECHR membership + Hague Convention accession places San Marino in the European Hague/ECHR intersection cluster.
Micro-state status (~34,000 population, ~61 km²) is structurally distinctive within the corpus.

sources.title: Order of Psychologists of San Marino

editorial_notes: San Marino jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 4 to 10 key_developments with full 301-CE-to-Capitani-Reggenti trajectory: 301-Republic-of-San-Marino-traditional-foundation + 1600-Statutes-of-1600 + 1862-Italo-Sammarinese-Treaty + 1944-Bombing-of-San-Marino-WWII + 1986-Law-51-1986-on-Family-Law + 1989-Council-of-Europe-membership + 1991-UN-membership-+-UNCRC + 2007-Hague-Convention-1980-accession + 2014-Law-49-2014-on-Filiation-+-same-sex-civil-union-2018 + 2024-Consiglio-dei-XII-+-Collegio-Garante-+-Capitani-Reggenti.
Civil-law Southern European micro-state (Law 51/1986 Family Law + Law 49/2014 Filiation + ECHR + Hague Convention 1980 accession 2007).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive superior-interest-of-the-child analysis under Law 51/1986 + Law 49/2014 + Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Southern-European + civil-law + Italian-derivative + ECHR + Hague-Convention + world's-oldest-extant-republic-traditionally-dated-301-CE-1725-years-globally-distinctive + Statutes-of-1600-oldest-still-in-force-constitutional-document-425-years-globally-distinctive + micro-state-34000-population-61-km2 + bombing-of-San-Marino-1944-WWII-neutrality-bombed-distinctive + Italo-Sammarinese-Treaty-1862-+-1939-amendments + Law-51-1986-Family-Law + Law-147-2018-same-sex-civil-union-Catholic-micro-state-distinctive + Capitani-Reggenti-semi-annually-rotating-Heads-of-State-distinctive clusters within the corpus.
